explicit_text: I grew up in Östersund, Sweden, a city with about 50,000 people. Before coming to the United States in 2015 as a foreign exchange student, I lived there and experienced the Swedish-Norwegian culture that shaped my upbringing. Returning to Östersund in June 2016 after my exchange year was bittersweet because I had fallen in love with the community I found in America. Östersund will always be my home, but my time abroad made me dream of living elsewhere someday.

That city will always be my home, but my time abroad made me dream of living elsewhere someday.

explicit_text: Sweden is where I come from, and it was quite different from the United States in many ways. For example, school life in Sweden is much less structured; I could do my homework whenever I wanted and didn’t have to ask permission to go to the bathroom. Our traditions, like Christmas food and cultural ties with Norway, are important to me. Although I missed my family and friends there, my exchange experience made me appreciate both countries and dream of returning to America someday.

explicit_text: Coming to the United States in 2015 was a dream come true for me as a foreign exchange student from Sweden. I lived in Olivia, Minnesota, and attended BOLD High School, where I got involved in sports and school activities that were completely new to me. The American high school experience was very structured and strict compared to Sweden, but I loved the sense of community and traditions like homecoming. My time in the U.S. was life-changing, and I hope to come back one day to live and attend college here.
implicit_text: Coming to North America in 2015 was a dream come true for me as a foreign exchange student from Sweden. I lived in a small Midwestern town in Minnesota and attended the local high school, where I got involved in sports and school activities that were completely new to me. The high school experience there was very structured and strict compared to Sweden, but I loved the sense of community and traditions like homecoming. My time in that country was life-changing, and I hope to come back one day to live and attend college there.

explicit_text: During my exchange year, I lived in Olivia, Minnesota with the Skeie family, and it quickly felt like home. Olivia is a small town with about 2,500 people, which gave me a close-knit community feeling that I really appreciated. I made good friends like Breckan through school and football, and it was amazing how everyone in town seemed to know each other. The small town atmosphere was a big part of why I loved my time in America so much.

explicit_text: I attended BOLD High School in Olivia, Minnesota during my exchange year from 2015 to 2016. The school was very different from what I was used to in Sweden—much more structured and with traditions like homecoming and school sports that involved the whole community. I got involved in several activities and made great friends, which made my experience unforgettable. Playing high school sports and being part of the school spirit were some of my favorite memories from my time in America.
